module-0-inventory-intake-batch.label: Module 0 Project: New Inventory Intake
module-0-inventory-intake-batch.summary: Add a five-item approved inventory batch through five cumulative INSERT steps.

ci_module_0_step2.title: Record an Item with No Notes

ci_module_0_step4.title: Add Two Items as One Batch

ci_module_0_step1.title: Add the First Approved Item
ci_module_0_step1.prompt: The fulfillment team has approved the first item for its new packing station. In `query.sql`, insert `id 101` with name `Desk Organizer`, category `Office`, price `12.99`, status `active`, `is_test = 0`, notes `New stock`, and `last_updated = '2026-02-10'`. Use an explicit column list, then verify the new row with `SELECT * FROM inventory_items WHERE id = 101`.
ci_module_0_step1.hint: This is the first project step. Build a clean starting version, then run it before moving on.

help.concept: This module project is progressive. Each step starts from the previous working solution and adds one focused feature.
help.hint_1: This is the first project step. Build a clean starting version, then run it before moving on.
help.hint_2: Click Run query and compare the results table with the expected result.

ci_module_0_step2.title: Record an Item with No Notes
ci_module_0_step2.prompt: A second approved item has no notes on the intake sheet. Keep the project moving by inserting `id 102`, `Cable Labels`, category `Office`, price `5.99`, status `active`, `is_test = 0`, SQL `NULL` for `notes`, and `last_updated = '2026-02-11'`. Verify only row `102` after the insert.
ci_module_0_step2.hint: Begin with the working code from the previous step. Keep that code, follow the new comments, and add only the next focused behavior.

help.concept: This module project is progressive. Each step starts from the previous working solution and adds one focused feature.
help.hint_1: Begin with the working code from the previous step. Keep that code, follow the new comments, and add only the next focused behavior.
help.hint_2: Run the previous working code first, then make only the focused change for this step.

ci_module_0_step2.starterCode:
-- Insert the first approved item
INSERT INTO inventory_items (id, name, category, price, status, is_test, notes, last_updated)
VALUES
  (101, 'Desk Organizer', 'Office', 12.99, 'active', 0, 'New stock', '2026-02-10');

-- Verify the insertion
SELECT * FROM inventory_items WHERE id = 101;
-- Project step 2: Record an Item with No Notes
-- Keep the working code above from the previous step.
-- Next, Add 'Cable Labels' to the inventory, ensuring the notes column is set to SQL NULL
-- Add only the focused change for this step inside the existing work.

query_sql.content:
-- Insert the first approved item
INSERT INTO inventory_items (id, name, category, price, status, is_test, notes, last_updated)
VALUES
  (101, 'Desk Organizer', 'Office', 12.99, 'active', 0, 'New stock', '2026-02-10');

-- Verify the insertion
SELECT * FROM inventory_items WHERE id = 101;
-- Project step 2: Record an Item with No Notes
-- Keep the working code above from the previous step.
-- Next, Add 'Cable Labels' to the inventory, ensuring the notes column is set to SQL NULL
-- Add only the focused change for this step inside the existing work.

ci_module_0_step3.title: Use the Approved Defaults
ci_module_0_step3.prompt: The Shipping Pouch should receive the database's approved defaults instead of hard-coded values. Insert `id 103`, name `Shipping Pouch`, category `Office`, price `3.49`, `is_test = 0`, and notes `Durable material`. Omit `status` and `last_updated` from the column list, then select row `103` to confirm the insert.
ci_module_0_step3.hint: Begin with the working code from the previous step. Keep that code, follow the new comments, and add only the next focused behavior.

ci_module_0_step4.title: Add Two Items as One Batch
ci_module_0_step4.prompt: Two small packing supplies were approved together and should be recorded as one batch. Use one multi-row `INSERT` to add `id 104`, `Packing Tape`, price `2.99`, notes `Strong adhesive`, and `id 105`, `Label Roll`, price `1.99`, notes `Easy to peel`. Both rows use category `Office`, status `active`, `is_test = 0`, and `last_updated = '2026-02-12'`. Verify IDs `104` and `105`.
ci_module_0_step4.hint: Begin with the working code from the previous step. Keep that code, follow the new comments, and add only the next focused behavior.

-- Verify the insertion
SELECT * FROM inventory_items WHERE id = 103;
-- Project step 4: Add Two Items as One Batch

ci_module_0_step4.solutionCode:
-- Insert the fourth and fifth approved items
INSERT INTO inventory_items (id, name, category, price, status, is_test, notes, last_updated)
VALUES
  (104, 'Packing Tape', 'Office', 2.99, 'active', 0, 'Strong adhesive', '2026-02-12'),
  (105, 'Label Roll', 'Office', 1.99, 'active', 0, 'Easy to peel', '2026-02-12');

-- Verify the insertion
SELECT * FROM inventory_items WHERE id IN (104, 105);

ci_module_0_step5.title: Finish the Intake Handoff
ci_module_0_step5.prompt: The intake script is ready for an operations handoff. Return every column from `inventory_items` and sort the complete inventory by `id`, so the reviewer can confirm the five new records with IDs `101` through `105` alongside the rows that already existed.
ci_module_0_step5.hint: Begin with the working code from the previous step. Keep that code, follow the new comments, and add only the next focused behavior.

INSERT INTO inventory_items (id, name, category, price, status, is_test, notes, last_updated)
VALUES
  (104, 'Packing Tape', 'Office', 2.99, 'active', 0, 'Strong adhesive', '2026-02-12'),
  (105, 'Label Roll', 'Office', 1.99, 'active', 0, 'Easy to peel', '2026-02-12');

-- Verify the insertion
SELECT * FROM inventory_items WHERE id IN (104, 105);
-- Project step 5: Finish the Intake Handoff
-- Keep the working code above from the previous step.
-- Next, Ensure all five new items are inserted correctly and verify the entire inventory remains consistent
-- Add only the focused change for this step inside the existing work.

SELECT * FROM inventory_items ORDER BY id;

sketch-module-0-intro.title: New Inventory Intake Project
sketch-module-0-intro.bodyMarkdown:
A small makers-market fulfillment team is opening a new packing station. You are the junior data operations analyst responsible for turning its approved five-item intake sheet into a safe SQL script.

Across the project, you will add `Desk Organizer`, `Cable Labels`, `Shipping Pouch`, `Packing Tape`, and `Label Roll` to `inventory_items`. Each step introduces one operational requirement: explicit column lists, SQL `NULL`, table defaults, a multi-row batch, and a final verification report. Existing inventory must remain untouched.

Your deliverable is a reviewed `query.sql` intake script that records IDs `101` through `105` with the approved values and ends with the complete inventory sorted by `id`.
